scores.materiality_reasoning: Land and compensation for confiscated property are direct material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property compensation is a broad legal right following civil unrest.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Recovered land remains a permanent asset for the citizen.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Isocrates discusses laws active during his lifetime in post-restoration Athens.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Claims the law applied to 'all the rest' as a matter of fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently define one as a victim of confiscation.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to execute a valid will controls the transmission of material wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Testamentary rights are a major structural legal category.
scores.persistence_reasoning: A valid will determines the lifelong afterlife of an estate.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Resident alien (metic) status is durable but changeable by moving.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary Aeginetan law during a current trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The verbatim asks to read the actual statute as proof.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Resident status does not define the specific local validity of a will.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Adoption is a legal procedure with significant inheritance consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly focused on the eligibility criteria for adoption.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Adoption creates a permanent, lifelong familial and legal bond.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Fellow-citizenship is a birth-fixed or hard-to-change legal status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a law currently in force in Aegina/Siphnos.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Explicitly cites the text of the law and its 'insistence'.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The rule 'only citizens can be adopted as citizens' is largely tautological.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Appointing heirs allows for the distribution of tangible estates and wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Foundational societal axis involving inheritance and family preservation.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The appointment of an heir has permanent effects on the estate's future.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Childlessness is a biological fact or state at a given moment.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes active laws regarding disposal of property.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The author encourages upholding an existing, functional law.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being childless does not naturally imply the legal right to adopt.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Inheriting property is a primary mode of material wealth transfer.
scores.generality_reasoning: Inheritance is a foundational societal right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Inheritance is a lifelong, structural transfer of status and assets.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Legitimacy is a birth-fixed status defined by lineage.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary family law and inheritance practices.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as an uncontested legal fact of the marriage.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Legitimacy is often defined by the right to inherit; thus tautological.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from inheritance is a major material deprivation.
scores.generality_reasoning: Inheritance exclusion is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loss of inheritance rights is typically lifelong and structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Illegitimacy is a birth-fixed status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary legal consequences of non-recognition.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Narrates the factual outcome of non-recognition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Illegitimacy is essentially defined by lack of inheritance/recognition rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exemption from military hazards and work affects bodily safety and effort.
scores.generality_reasoning: Exemption from labor and war is a broad structural privilege.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Priestly status and its exemptions are lifelong in this context.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Hereditary priesthood is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes Egyptian customs from a legendary/historical distance.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports laws as established facts of the Egyptian system.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Priesthood is a role; military exemption is a specific legal corollary.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Affluence and sacrifice revenues are tangible wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the economic rights of a religious caste.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Professional affluence is a lifelong structural condition.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Egyptian priesthood is a fixed structural role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses the quasi-mythical Busiris and his foundation of Egypt.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the system's economic logic as an established fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a priest does not inherently define one as 'affluent' by revenue.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom of movement is a core material liberty.
scores.generality_reasoning: Restriction on travel is a broad category of civil control.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Restriction lasts while the citizen is of military age.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Being 'fit for military service' is an age-based biological grouping.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to well-known Spartan practices of the author's own time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Identifies a specific 'provision' as an established fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Military fitness does not automatically define legal travel restrictions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection of physical goods and property is highly material.
scores.generality_reasoning: Property protection is a broad foundational axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Provides a life-long structural arrangement for economic safety.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Work/laborer status is a durable occupation-based group.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes idealised/historical Egyptian laws.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Isocrates proposes adopting these laws, implying a mix of fact and wish.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a worker does not definitionally include being protected by soldiers.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right (or lack thereof) to change jobs affects economic choices.
scores.generality_reasoning: Focuses on the specific regulation of occupational mobility.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The rule mandates that individuals stay in one job for life.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Caste/guild membership in Egypt was a structural/hereditary status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes ancient Egyptian social structure.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the 'orders' of Busiris as factual foundations of the state.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a 'worker' does not inherently mean one is barred from switching.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Charge of important matters equals control over state resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political power is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lasts from the onset of 'old age' until death.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Age is a biologically fixed, immutable category at any given time.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the legendary foundation of the Egyptian state.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Narrated as a specific appointment/law by the founder.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Old age is not defined by the possession of political power.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Addressing the assembly has direct effects on laws and state wealth.
scores.generality_reasoning: Political speech (isegoria) is a foundational civic right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The right to address the assembly is a lifelong citizen privilege.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a hard-to-change legal birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Isocrates is currently performing the action in the Athenian context.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim describes the speaker exercising an accepted duty/right.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Civic belonging does not tautologically include the right to speak.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Public subsidies (theorika/refuge) are tangible material resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Access to welfare/safety reflects a broad category of state service.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Subsidies are ongoing but dependent on the city's prosperity.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the benefits of the current Athenian system.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports these services as a factual adaptation of the city to its people.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not define one as a recipient of subsidies.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from corporal punishment/slavery is a bodily entitlement.
scores.generality_reasoning: Freedom from physical coercion is a foundational societal right.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Slave status is generally lifelong and structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slavery is a hard-to-change legal status (requires manumission).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses common contemporary Greek attitudes toward household slaves.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports the practice of enslaving barbarians as a matter of fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slavery is definitionally the lack of freedom and physical autonomy.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from public office deprives one of political agency.
scores.generality_reasoning: Holding office is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Metic status (excluded from office) is usually lifelong in Athens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic status is a durable social identity, though changeable by move.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the legal realities of political participation in current polities.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Describes the legal robbing of government shares as a known fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an alien resident does not definitionally include a ban on office.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to sacred rites is primarily a symbolic/religious resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Tied to a specific recurring ritual (Eleusinian Mysteries).
scores.persistence_reasoning: The ban is 'for ever', making it a lifelong structural exclusion.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ethnicity (Barbarian) is a biologically fixed/birth-fixed status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to current proclamations at the contemporary Mysteries.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Cites a specific, attested formal warning by priestly families.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a foreigner does not define exclusion from every religious rite.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Honor and prestige are symbolic/immaterial resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Power in cities reflects a broad class of life outcomes.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Education-based status holds as long as the skill is maintained.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Education/skill is changeable with effort but defines a durable identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to the current value of Greek education/eloquence.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: The claim is rhetorical, arguing for the prestige of the author's craft.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Eloquence is a skill; honor is an external social reward, not a definition.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Freedom of speech (parrhesia) allows influence over material life and law.
scores.generality_reasoning: Freedom of speech is a foundational axis of Greek civic life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Status as a 'private citizen' lasts while not in magistracy or royalty.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizen status is structural birth-status.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the current legal/civic environment of contemporary Greece.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Lists 'freedom of speech' as a factual part of the legal environment.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a private citizen does not definitionally include free speech rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Honest admonition and critical feedback are abstract/trait-based resources.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring specific social interaction in courtly settings.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Structural isolation lasts for the duration of the king's reign.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Kingship is a structural birth-status or hard-to-change role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Isocrates is writing to a contemporary king about his current situation.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: An analytical/rhetorical claim about the nature of royal social circles.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Royalty is defined by total power, not necessarily by isolation.
